description_long: This object resembles a Madagascar hissing cockroach, featuring a robust, oval-shaped body with a distinct segmentation between the dark, glossy cephalothorax and the lighter, banded abdomen. The exoskeleton is hard and slightly shiny, with a gradient of colors from deep black at the head to a warm, reddish-brown and tan along the abdomen. The legs are spindly, black, and jointed, with fine details visible in their articulation. The head is small and partially tucked under the pronotum, with no visible claws but some short, fine antennae extending forward. The surface texture appears smooth but with subtle ridges and bumps, especially along the abdomen. This crustacean would be found scuttling across urban environments, blending into leaf litter or debris.
description: A robust, oval-bodied crustacean with a glossy black head and banded, reddish-brown abdomen, featuring spindly black legs and a hard, segmented exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Side view showing the pronounced segmentation between the dark cephalothorax and the lighter, banded abdomen, with visible spindly legs.
description_view_1: Dorsal view highlighting the full width of the banded abdomen and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with clear visibility of the segmentation and the curvature of the body.
description_view_3: Frontal view showing the small, dark head partially hidden under the pronotum, with antennae faintly visible.
